items.headline: City Council to study Health and Safety Element update for General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to hold a study session on updating the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (covering 2015–2040). This element typically addresses emergency preparedness, hazard mitigation, public health infrastructure, and safety standards that shape land use and development decisions.

items.headline: Two commercial solar+battery projects permitted across San José
items.summary: Roof-mounted solar and battery storage systems received permits at 875 N 13th St ($665,633) and 55 Old Tully Rd ($643,960). These back-to-back commercial energy projects suggest growing adoption of on-site renewable power and storage in the city.

items.headline: New single-family home permitted on Saratoga Avenue
items.summary: A new house with attached garage received a permit at 1489 Saratoga Av for $549,610. This is the only new single-family residential construction in this batch, distinct from the five ADU conversions also permitted this week.
